Later beads\n(e.g. the blob-identity runtime checks under **bf-f841**) and reruns should start\nfrom here instead of re-deriving the command sequence or the tuning.\n\n---\n\n## TL;DR\n\n```bash\n./scripts/run-sim-local.sh\n# expects, within ~30s:\n#   [run-sim] mothership healthy\n#     t= 1s  nodes_online=4  blobs=2  peak=2\n#     ...\n#   [run-sim] peak_blob_count=2\n#   [run-sim] PASS: >=1 blob via /api/blobs (peak=2) AND sim --verify passed\n```\n\nExit 0 iff ≥1 tracked blob is observed via `GET /api/blobs` over the run window.\n\n---\n\n## What the script does (the exact reproduce sequence)\n\n1. **Build mothership from source** — `cd mothership && go build -o /tmp/spaxel-mothership ./cmd/mothership`\n2. **Build the canonical sim from source** — `cd mothership && go build -o /tmp/spaxel-sim ./cmd/sim`\n3. **Start the mothership** on an ephemeral port + data dir:\n   ```bash\n   SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8088 \\\n   SPAXEL_DATA_DIR=$(mktemp -d) \\\n   SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=warn \\\n   SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED=false \\\n   TZ=UTC \\\n     /tmp/spaxel-mothership &\n   ```\n4. **Wait for health** — poll `GET /healthz` until `{\"status\":\"ok\"}`.\n5. **Run the sim** with `--verify`:\n   ```bash\n   /tmp/spaxel-sim \\\n     --mothership ws://localhost:8088/ws/node \\\n     --nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --space 5x5x2.5 \\\n     --duration 25 --seed 42 --verify\n   ```\n6. **Observe** — poll `GET /api/blobs` once per second for the whole streaming\n   window, keep the peak, gate on `peak ≥ 1`. (`sim --verify` is a reported\n   secondary cross-check that asserts `blob_count == walkers ±1`.)\n\nEach step is in `scripts/run-sim-local.sh` with a `trap` cleanup that kills both\nprocesses and removes the temp data dir.\n\n---\n\n## The two gotchas (read these before touching the path)\n\n### 1. There are TWO `cmd/sim` packages — only one has `--verify`\n\n| Path | Module | Has `--verify`? | Use it? |\n|------|--------|-----------------|---------|\n| `mothership/cmd/sim/` | part of the mothership module | **YES** (`verify.go`) | **YES — canonical** |\n| `cmd/sim/` | separate, older module (own `go.mod`) | no | **NO** |\n\n`scripts/run-sim-local.sh` builds **only** from `mothership/cmd/sim` and\nadditionally guards against a stale binary by checking `--help` for `--verify`,\nrebuilding if missing. **Do not** build/run `cmd/sim` for runtime blob checks.\n\n### 2. `rate=30`, not the upstream default `20`\n\nAt the upstream default `--rate 20`, fusion produces a peak only *while* a walker\nmoves enough to cross the DeltaRMS threshold, so a run can legitimately land\n`peak=2` *or* `peak=0` nondeterministically (see the comment in\n`mothership/cmd/sim/verify.go`). Bumping `--rate` to **30** reliably clears the\nthreshold: every observed run hits `peak == walkers` with the first blob at ~1s.\nThis is a rate/tuning fact, **not** a broken pipeline — if a bead reports 0 blobs,\nfirst re-run at `rate=30` before suspecting a defect.\n\n`--duration 25` (not 40) keeps the sim's late-window `--verify` sample inside the\nactive-walk phase; at long durations walkers drift into low-signal positions and\nverify can spuriously report 0 even though the `/api/blobs` window still saw blobs.\n\n---\n\n## Token path (no manual token needed)\n\nThe canonical sim authenticates exactly like real provisioned firmware:\n\n- `--token` defaults to empty → the sim calls `POST /api/provision` to mint a real\n  per-node HMAC token (`HMAC-SHA256(install_secret, node_mac)`), sent as the\n  `X-Spaxel-Token` header on the WebSocket dial, then bridged into `hello.Token`\n  for validation (bf-1o7qi).\n- Fallback: if provisioning fails, the sim generates a synthetic token. Either\n  way the connection is not left tokenless-by-design.\n- Defense-in-depth: the mothership also tolerates tokenless nodes for\n  `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS` (default 24h) after startup, so even a token\n  glitch during a reproduce run does not cause a `reject` (bf-2hdbg). The script\n  counts `reject`/`401`/`403` lines in the sim log and reports the count; expect 0.\n\nIf you ever see rejects, check `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS` (the e2e harness\nsets it to `0` to force strict validation) and that the binary is built from\n`mothership/cmd/sim`.\n\n---\n\n## Environment (mothership)\n\n| Variable | Value in the script | Why |\n|----------|---------------------|-----|\n| `SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR` | `127.0.0.1:8088` | Loopback; avoid clashing with anything on 8080 |\n| `SPAXEL_DATA_DIR` | ephemeral `mktemp -d` | Clean state every run; removed on exit |\n| `SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL` | `warn` | Quiet enough to scan, loud enough on real problems |\n| `SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED` | `false` | Loopback reproduce needs no multicast; sim dials directly |\n| `TZ` | `UTC` | Deterministic diurnal/timestamp behavior |","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"dfb67b70-e8bc-432b-bad2-0aac16df4a69","uuid":"ffdd0436-6112-4f58-9269-2e1313e16fce","timestamp":"2026-07-08T09:43:50.325Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff --git a/notes/hardware-free-runtime.md b/notes/hardware-free-runtime.md\nindex 532402f..11ac4c7 100644\n--- a/notes/hardware-free-runtime.md\n+++ b/notes/hardware-free-runtime.md\n@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@\n # Hardware-Free Runtime Reproduce\n \n **Canonical script:** `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`\n-**Verified:** 2026-07-08 — `5bbe52c` (post bf-243os sim→blob path fix) and re-verified at HEAD `f859f3e` for bf-40hc acceptance.\n+**Verified:** 2026-07-08 — `5bbe52c` (post bf-243os sim→blob path fix), re-verified at HEAD `f859f3e` for bf-40hc acceptance, and final-clean-rebuild re-verified at HEAD `c927f67` (the bf-40hc doc commit itself).\n **Resolves:** the ambiguity that caused bf-40hc's repeated failures; closes the chain bf-3zll → bf-3hji → bf-51fq → bf-5cr3, and **satisfies bf-40hc** (hardware-free runtime path that creates a blob).\n \n This is the single deterministic artifact that captures the working end-to-end\n===CURRENT NOTES FILE===\n# Hardware-Free Runtime Reproduce\n\n**Canonical script:** `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`\n**Verified:** 2026-07-08 — `5bbe52c` (post bf-243os sim→blob path fix), re-verified at HEAD `f859f3e` for bf-40hc acceptance, and final-clean-rebuild re-verified at HEAD `c927f67` (the bf-40hc doc commit itself).\n**Resolves:** the ambiguity that caused bf-40hc's repeated failures; There are TWO `cmd/sim` packages — only one has `--verify`\n\n| Path | Module | Has `--verify`? | Use it? |\n|------|--------|-----------------|---------|\n| `mothership/cmd/sim/` | part of the mothership module | **YES** (`verify.go`) | **YES — canonical** |\n| `cmd/sim/` | separate, older module (own `go.mod`) | no | **NO** |\n\n`scripts/run-sim-local.sh` builds **only** from `mothership/cmd/sim` and\nadditionally guards against a stale binary by checking `--help` for `--verify`,\nrebuilding if missing. **Do not** build/run `cmd/sim` for runtime blob checks.\n\n### 2. `rate=30`, not the upstream default `20`\n\nAt the upstream default `--rate 20`, fusion produces a peak only *while* a walker\nmoves enough to cross the DeltaRMS threshold, so a run can legitimately land\n`peak=2` *or* `peak=0` nondeterministically (see the comment in\n`mothership/cmd/sim/verify.go`). {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_3852d7caf0684382b3ce08ad","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nname: auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads\ndescription: \"Auto-Split dispatch ('bead failed N times, split it') can fire on beads whose work is actually DONE but stuck in_progress — verify + close, don't split\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 96b52212-e56d-4bfe-8793-3862c103275a\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` **Auto-Split: Decompose This Bead** dispatch fires on any bead stuck `in_progress` with `failure-count:3`, instructing you to create 3-5 child beads. But that signal alone does NOT mean the work failed — the bead can be **complete-but-stuck-open**, typically because prior `br close` attempts silently failed (see [[auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid]] and [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]). In that case splitting just re-does already-finished work and pollutes the bead graph.\n\n**Concrete case (bf-34lwt, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired asking to re-investigate a REJECT/token root cause. But the bead was already split into 3 closed children (bf-5ig3e/bf-29wyl/bf-2hdbg), had its deliverable posted on the parent (bf-4iewr) **three times** as comments, plus a committed write-up (`docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md`), and had been re-verified at HEAD twice. It was stuck open only because the templated `br close --body` kept failing. `br close bf-34lwt` (no flags) closed it instantly and broke the loop.\n\n**How to apply — before honoring an Auto-Split dispatch, check whether the work is already done:**\n1. `br comments list <parent-of-this-bead>` — is the deliverable already posted?\n2. `git log --oneline -- <id>` and `git log --oneline | grep <id>` — prior close/verify commits?\n3. Are its existing children already `closed` (`br show <each>`)?\n4. Does the committed artifact exist?\n\nIf the work is verifiably complete: post a \"already done, closing to break stuck-close loop\" comment, then `br close <id>` (no `--body`), then `br sync --flush-only`. Do NOT split. Do NOT emit `SPLIT_COMPLETE`. If genuinely incomplete, then split per the dispatch.\n\n**Concrete case (bf-4mle6, 2026-07-07) — the OTHER branch (genuinely incomplete, so DO split, but scope to gaps):** Auto-split fired on a bead with 372 lines of *uncommitted* implementation in the working tree (`cmd/sim/main.go` provisioning real tokens via `/api/provision`) — but its acceptance criteria were NOT met: PROGRESS.md still described the now-obsolete old policy, nothing verified behavior under `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0`, and the e2e diff was *mixed* with unrelated bf-4q5w (IO-6 hard-gate) test code. **How to apply when incomplete-but-already-implemented:** split into children scoped to the REMAINING GAPS (verify the claim → fix/test the harness → document+commit+close), and put \"the implementation ALREADY EXISTS in the working tree — do NOT re-implement\" at the top of the first child so no child re-does written code. Add a child to DISENTANGLE unrelated work mixed into the same diff (it belongs to a different bead — keep it out of this bead's commit/acceptance). Don't close incomplete/uncommitted work; the \"failures\" are usually the stuck-close symptom, not a sign the task is too big.\n\n**THIRD branch — failure-count from TIMEOUTS, not incompleteness (bf-20q9o, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired on a broad *diagnostic* bead (\"pin where sim CSI is dropped in the fusion path\") at failure-count:3. But the metadata in `.beads/traces/bf-20q9o/metadata.json` showed all 3 \"failures\" were `exit_code:124, outcome:timeout, duration_ms:600001` — the agent kept hitting the 10-min ceiling mid-investigation, NOT failing the task. The 10MB `stdout.txt` trace held a **near-complete root-cause finding** the prior run had reached but never posted (it timed out one verification away). **How to apply when failures are timeouts on a diagnostic/research bead:** before splitting, (1) read `metadata.json` — if `outcome:timeout`, the prior run may have the answer; (2) parse the trace's `text_delta` (assistant visible text) and `tool_result`/`file_path` entries with a small python json-stream reader (NOT thinking deltas — those are noise); (3) if a concrete finding exists, **verify it at HEAD yourself** (don't trust the trace secondhand — re-Read the cited `file:line`), then post it as a comment + `br close` (no `--body`). Splitting would discard a 90%-done diagnosis and fragment a solved problem across children that would just re-derive what the trace already holds. For bf-20q9o the trace's finding (colon-vs-dash delimiter mismatch between `CSIFrame.LinkID()` frame.go:119-121 and `splitLinkID` main.go:5407-5415) verified clean at HEAD → posted comment 144 → closed. Close hit [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] (4 naive rows from the timeout dispatches) — one Nix-sqlite3 repair unblocked it. **Distinguishing the three branches:** (a) work posted/committed but stuck-open on a close failure → just close; (b) work implemented-but-uncommitted in the tree → commit + close (or split to remaining gaps); (c) work found-but-unposted in a *timeout* trace → verify at HEAD + post + close.\n\n**FOURTH branch — failure-count accrued entirely while BLOCKED on a dependency (bf-1r1ya, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired at failure-count:5 on \"Get Fuse to emit >=1 peak — fix sim CSI amplitude to clear minDelta.\" But the bead's body said it was BLOCKED on child bf-561zr (splitLinkID delimiter fix), and `br show bf-561zr` was `closed` — having just landed at HEAD (`git log` showed `b9f362c fix(bf-561zr): ...` minutes earlier). All 5 \"failures\" happened while the blocker was open, i.e. before the work could even be attempted — so failure-count was a blocked-period artifact, NOT a complexity signal. **How to apply when the bead has a just-landed blocker:** before splitting, (1) `br show <this-bead>` — does the body name a dependency? Is it now `closed`? (2) `git log --oneline | grep <blocker-id>` — did it land recently? If yes, the real task is newly unblocked and may be trivially satisfiable: **run it yourself at HEAD.** For bf-1r1ya: rebuilt mothership (binaries predated the fix), ran spaxel-sim 25s, and the FUSE-DBG log showed `links=12 deltaOK=4..8 (minDelta=0.01 dRange=0.14..16.08) active=4..8` every tick — i.e. the gate was cleared by 14–1600× on every fusion tick, no amplitude change needed. All 3 acceptance criteria met by existing code → posted FUSE-DBG evidence as comment + `br close` (no --body) → closed. Splitting would have fabricated 5 children for a task that was already done the moment its blocker landed. **Distinguishing this branch:** the diagnostic is *temporal* — were the failures during a period when a named dependency was still open? If so, re-run the now-unblocked task before trusting failure-count as a complexity measure. (Close again hit [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]: 6+ naive length-19 `claimed_at` rows from today's dispatches — one scoped Nix-sqlite3 `UPDATE ... WHERE bead_id='bf-1r1ya'` repair unblocked it.) So the four branches are now: (a) posted/committed, stuck on close failure → just close; (b) implemented-but-uncommitted → commit + close; (c) found-in-timeout-trace → verify at HEAD + post + close; (d) **failures-while-blocked, blocker just landed → re-run at HEAD + close (often no split, no code change).**\n\n**RESOLUTION of the bf-4mle6 case (2026-07-07, same day):** The legitimate split WAS done — children bf-1o7qi (header→hello.Token bridge, committed 9c38cbb), bf-qzrmq (harness under SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0, committed 250056c), bf-4q5w/bf-f0jee (disentangled IO-6 hard-gate test code, committed c534144/a06586c). But then Auto-Split **re-fired on the FINAL \"document + commit + close\" child (bf-42cao) at failure-count:3** — even though that child's work (PROGRESS.md window-independent policy write-up + cmd/sim/main.go `--provision` impl) was verifiably COMPLETE but uncommitted. Resolved by: `go build/vet ./cmd/sim` clean → committed ONLY cmd/sim/main.go + PROGRESS.md (deliberately EXCLUDING bf-4q5w's e2e_test.go code, which belongs to the still-open bf-4q5w) as `feat(sim): provision real per-node HMAC token by default (bf-4mle6)` → `br comments add` then `br close` (no --body) on bf-4mle6 AND bf-42cao → flush. bf-42cao's close hit [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] (recurring: 9 naive `worker_sessions.claimed_at` rows from today's dispatches) — one Nix-sqlite3 repair unblocked it. **Reinforced lesson:** the *final commit+close child* of a legitimate split is the MOST prone to spurious re-split — it's a tiny task that gets stuck on the `br close --body`/claimed_at failure, accumulates failure-count:3, and looks \"too big\" to the dispatcher when it's actually done. When Auto-Split fires on a child whose title is \"commit and close <parent>\", check the working tree FIRST: if the deliverable is already written (especially a PROGRESS.md/doc change), commit the focused set (using explicit paths so unrelated beads' diffs stay out) and close — do NOT split, do NOT emit SPLIT_COMPLETE. Note bf-4iewr (umbrella) stayed open: it has a second open child (bf-601bz, live two-boot stderr-grep verification) not satisfied by code alone.\n===CLOSE-BODY===\n---\nname: auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid\ndescription: \"Auto-dispatch bead templates say `br close <id> --body`, but that arg is invalid — use comments add + close\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 2d260119-d2dc-4d0d-8b73-c865556c7fc4\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` dispatch footer instructs: `br close <id> --body \"brief summary\"`. bead-forge's `br close` does NOT accept `--body` (only `--reason`, default \"Completed\"). Blindly running the templated command fails with `unexpected argument '--body'` and climbs the bead's `failure-count` (bf-2fz8 had failure-count:2 for exactly this reason).\n\n**Why:** The dispatch template's close syntax is wrong for this `br` build; it is not the same failure mode as the historical [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] timestamp corruption (that one errored \"premature end of input\"; bf-2fz8's `br close` ran cleanly once the body was attached separately).\n\n**How to apply:** When closing an auto-dispatched bead, attach the summary as a comment first, then close with no body flag:\n1. `br comments add <id> \"<summary text>\"`  (variadic positional TEXT — run from the repo root where `.beads/` lives, or it errors \"No .beads directory\")\n2. `br close <id>`\n3. `br sync --flush-only` (mutations don't auto-flush; checkpoint the close)\n\nAcceptance criteria phrased as \"record the finding as a bead comment\" are satisfied by step 1.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"dfb67b70-e8bc-432b-bad2-0aac16df4a69","uuid":"06c3f15e-8833-49cc-8667-da42ef52467c","timestamp":"2026-07-08T09:44:50.041Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"---\nname: auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads\ndescription: \"Auto-Split dispatch ('bead failed N times, split it') can fire on beads whose work is actually DONE but stuck in_progress — verify + close, don't split\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 96b52212-e56d-4bfe-8793-3862c103275a\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` **Auto-Split: Decompose This Bead** dispatch fires on any bead stuck `in_progress` with `failure-count:3`, instructing you to create 3-5 child beads. Does the committed artifact exist?\n\nIf the work is verifiably complete: post a \"already done, closing to break stuck-close loop\" comment, then `br close <id>` (no `--body`), then `br sync --flush-only`. Do NOT split. Do NOT emit `SPLIT_COMPLETE`. If genuinely incomplete, then split per the dispatch.\n\n**Concrete case (bf-4mle6, 2026-07-07) — the OTHER branch (genuinely incomplete, so DO split, but scope to gaps):** Auto-split fired on a bead with 372 lines of *uncommitted* implementation in the working tree (`cmd/sim/main.go` provisioning real tokens via `/api/provision`) — but its acceptance criteria were NOT met: PROGRESS.md still described the now-obsolete old policy, nothing verified behavior under `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0`, and the e2e diff was *mixed* with unrelated bf-4q5w (IO-6 hard-gate) test code. **How to apply when incomplete-but-already-implemented:** split into children scoped to the REMAINING GAPS (verify the claim → fix/test the harness → document+commit+close), and put \"the implementation ALREADY EXISTS in the working tree — do NOT re-implement\" at the top of the first child so no child re-does written code. Add a child to DISENTANGLE unrelated work mixed into the same diff (it belongs to a different bead — keep it out of this bead's commit/acceptance). Don't close incomplete/uncommitted work; the \"failures\" are usually the stuck-close symptom, not a sign the task is too big.\n\n**THIRD branch — failure-count from TIMEOUTS, not incompleteness (bf-20q9o, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired on a broad *diagnostic* bead (\"pin where sim CSI is dropped in the fusion path\") at failure-count:3. But the metadata in `.beads/traces/bf-20q9o/metadata.json` showed all 3 \"failures\" were `exit_code:124, outcome:timeout, duration_ms:600001` — the agent kept hitting the 10-min ceiling mid-investigation, NOT failing the task. The 10MB `stdout.txt` trace held a **near-complete root-cause finding** the prior run had reached but never posted (it timed out one verification away). **How to apply when failures are timeouts on a diagnostic/research bead:** before splitting, (1) read `metadata.json` — if `outcome:timeout`, the prior run may have the answer; (2) parse the trace's `text_delta` (assistant visible text) and `tool_result`/`file_path` entries with a small python json-stream reader (NOT thinking deltas — those are noise); (3) if a concrete finding exists, **verify it at HEAD yourself** (don't trust the trace secondhand — re-Read the cited `file:line`), then post it as a comment + `br close` (no `--body`). Splitting would discard a 90%-done diagnosis and fragment a solved problem across children that would just re-derive what the trace already holds. For bf-20q9o the trace's finding (colon-vs-dash delimiter mismatch between `CSIFrame.LinkID()` frame.go:119-121 and `splitLinkID` main.go:5407-5415) verified clean at HEAD → posted comment 144 → closed. Close hit [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] (4 naive rows from the timeout dispatches) — one Nix-sqlite3 repair unblocked it. **Distinguishing the three branches:** (a) work posted/committed but stuck-open on a close failure → just close; (b) work implemented-but-uncommitted in the tree → commit + close (or split to remaining gaps); (c) work found-but-unposted in a *timeout* trace → verify at HEAD + post + close.\n\n**FOURTH branch — failure-count accrued entirely while BLOCKED on a dependency (bf-1r1ya, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired at failure-count:5 on \"Get Fuse to emit >=1 peak — fix sim CSI amplitude to clear minDelta.\" But the bead's body said it was BLOCKED on child bf-561zr (splitLinkID delimiter fix), and `br show bf-561zr` was `closed` — having just landed at HEAD (`git log` showed `b9f362c fix(bf-561zr): ...` minutes earlier). All 5 \"failures\" happened while the blocker was open, i.e. before the work could even be attempted — so failure-count was a blocked-period artifact, NOT a complexity signal. **How to apply when the bead has a just-landed blocker:** before splitting, (1) `br show <this-bead>` — does the body name a dependency? Is it now `closed`? (2) `git log --oneline | grep <blocker-id>` — did it land recently? If yes, the real task is newly unblocked and may be trivially satisfiable: **run it yourself at HEAD.** For bf-1r1ya: rebuilt mothership (binaries predated the fix), ran spaxel-sim 25s, and the FUSE-DBG log showed `links=12 deltaOK=4..8 (minDelta=0.01 dRange=0.14..16.08) active=4..8` every tick — i.e. the gate was cleared by 14–1600× on every fusion tick, no amplitude change needed. All 3 acceptance criteria met by existing code → posted FUSE-DBG evidence as comment + `br close` (no --body) → closed. Splitting would have fabricated 5 children for a task that was already done the moment its blocker landed. **Distinguishing this branch:** the diagnostic is *temporal* — were the failures during a period when a named dependency was still open? If so, re-run the now-unblocked task before trusting failure-count as a complexity measure. (Close again hit [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]: 6+ naive length-19 `claimed_at` rows from today's dispatches — one scoped Nix-sqlite3 `UPDATE ... WHERE bead_id='bf-1r1ya'` repair unblocked it.) So the four branches are now: (a) posted/committed, stuck on close failure → just close; (b) implemented-but-uncommitted → commit + close; (c) found-in-timeout-trace → verify at HEAD + post + close; (d) **failures-while-blocked, blocker just landed → re-run at HEAD + close (often no split, no code change).**\n\n**RESOLUTION of the bf-4mle6 case (2026-07-07, same day):** The legitimate split WAS done — children bf-1o7qi (header→hello.Token bridge, committed 9c38cbb), bf-qzrmq (harness under SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0, committed 250056c), bf-4q5w/bf-f0jee (disentangled IO-6 hard-gate test code, committed c534144/a06586c). But then Auto-Split **re-fired on the FINAL \"document + commit + close\" child (bf-42cao) at failure-count:3** — even though that child's work (PROGRESS.md window-independent policy write-up + cmd/sim/main.go `--provision` impl) was verifiably COMPLETE but uncommitted. Resolved by: `go build/vet ./cmd/sim` clean → committed ONLY cmd/sim/main.go + PROGRESS.md (deliberately EXCLUDING bf-4q5w's e2e_test.go code, which belongs to the still-open bf-4q5w) as `feat(sim): provision real per-node HMAC token by default (bf-4mle6)` → `br comments add` then `br close` (no --body) on bf-4mle6 AND bf-42cao → flush. bf-42cao's close hit [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] (recurring: 9 naive `worker_sessions.claimed_at` rows from today's dispatches) — one Nix-sqlite3 repair unblocked it. **Reinforced lesson:** the *final commit+close child* of a legitimate split is the MOST prone to spurious re-split — it's a tiny task that gets stuck on the `br close --body`/claimed_at failure, accumulates failure-count:3, and looks \"too big\" to the dispatcher when it's actually done. When Auto-Split fires on a child whose title is \"commit and close <parent>\", check the working tree FIRST: if the deliverable is already written (especially a PROGRESS.md/doc change), commit the focused set (using explicit paths so unrelated beads' diffs stay out) and close — do NOT split, do NOT emit SPLIT_COMPLETE. Note bf-4iewr (umbrella) stayed open: it has a second open child (bf-601bz, live two-boot stderr-grep verification) not satisfied by code alone.\n===CLOSE-BODY===\n---\nname: auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid\ndescription: \"Auto-dispatch bead templates say `br close <id> --body`, but that arg is invalid — use comments add + close\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 2d260119-d2dc-4d0d-8b73-c865556c7fc4\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` dispatch footer instructs: `br close <id> --body \"brief summary\"`. bead-forge's `br close` does NOT accept `--body` (only `--reason`, default \"Completed\"). Blindly running the templated command fails with `unexpected argument '--body'` and climbs the bead's `failure-count` (bf-2fz8 had failure-count:2 for exactly this reason).\n\n**Why:** The dispatch template's close syntax is wrong for this `br` build; it is not the same failure mode as the historical [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] timestamp corruption (that one errored \"premature end of input\"; bf-2fz8's `br close` ran cleanly once the body was attached separately).\n\n**How to apply:** When closing an auto-dispatched bead, attach the summary as a comment first, then close with no body flag:\n1. `br comments add <id> \"<summary text>\"`  (variadic positional TEXT — run from the repo root where `.beads/` lives, or it errors \"No .beads directory\")\n2. `br close <id>`\n3. `br sync --flush-only` (mutations don't auto-flush; checkpoint the close)\n\nAcceptance criteria phrased as \"record the finding as a bead comment\" are satisfied by step 1.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2d439466c35f44fabcca2249","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n```bash\nSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n```\n(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n\n**Confirmed again 2026-07-07** (bf-601bz: 4 length-19 rows blocked close; scoped the WHERE to `bead_id='<id>'` for a targeted fix). Both RFC3339 suffixes are accepted by `br close`: the `+00:00` form above AND trailing `Z` via `strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', claimed_at)` both worked.
